source_actor: Office of the Government of the Czech Republic
interaction_phrase: National Research, Development, and Innovation Policy of the Czech Republic 2021+13
occurrence_sentence: The National Strategy for Quantum Technologies is linked to the National Research, Development, and Innovation Policy of the Czech Republic 2021+13 by building on its priorities in the field of cutting-edge research, using its framework and funding instruments, supporting the development of human resources and knowledge transfer between academia and industry, and strengthening the internationalization of Czech research through participation in European and global initiatives.

interaction_phrase: the Czech Republic is a member through VŠB-TUO
occurrence_sentence: As part of the LUMI AI Factory consortium, of which the Czech Republic is a member through VŠB-TUO, the LUMI-IQ quantum computer will be purchased as an experimental AI platform, which will also be accessible to research communities from across the Czech Republic.

interaction_phrase: joined the European association
occurrence_sentence: In 2025, VŠB-TUO joined the European association QuIC (Quantum Industry Consortium), which includes more than 180 institutions and companies from across Europe and aims to accelerate the development of quantum technologies and strengthen Europe's position as a world leader in this rapidly growing sector.

interaction_phrase: issued a
occurrence_sentence: In 2024, the European Commission issued a "Recommendation on a coordinated implementation plan for the transition to post-quantum cryptography(45) ", and in 2025, NÚKIB joined the joint statement of EU member states on the transition to post-quantum cryptography, "Securing Tomorrow, Today: Transitioning to Post-Quantum Cryptography(46) ".

interaction_phrase: joined the joint statement of EU member states
occurrence_sentence: In 2024, the European Commission issued a "Recommendation on a coordinated implementation plan for the transition to post-quantum cryptography(45) ", and in 2025, NÚKIB joined the joint statement of EU member states on the transition to post-quantum cryptography, "Securing Tomorrow, Today: Transitioning to Post-Quantum Cryptography(46) ".

interaction_phrase: joined research leading to the construction of a secure quantum communication backbone
occurrence_sentence: Through the EuroQCI initiative, the Czech Republic has joined research leading to the construction of a secure quantum communication backbone network in the EU.

interaction_phrase: preparation of the European EU Quantum Flagship initiative
occurrence_sentence: In 2016, the Czech Republic launched the National Initiative for Quantum Technologies, which was launched in connection with the preparation of the European EU Quantum Flagship initiative and aims to promote and support the development of quantum technologies.

interaction_phrase: VŠB-TUO became a member of the European association QuIC
occurrence_sentence: In 2025, VŠB-TUO became a member of the European association QuIC (Quantum Industry Consortium), which seeks to accelerate the development of quantum technologies and strengthen Europe's role as a global leader in this dynamically developing field.

interaction_phrase: Department of Coherent Optics of the Institute of Scientific Instruments (ÚPT) of
occurrence_sentence: The Department of Coherent Optics of the Institute of Scientific Instruments (ÚPT) of the Czech Academy of Sciences is also involved in a number of consortium projects supporting both its own development of quantum ion clocks within the European metrology community and, above all, the implementation and expansion of a network of coherent transmission lines for the distribution of precise time derived from these clocks.

interaction_phrase: compared with other countries
occurrence_sentence: If we compare the Czech Republic with other countries in all contexts, such as the US, Australia, the UK, the Netherlands, Ireland, Denmark, or Spain, we see an opportunity to support the development of quantum technologies, especially in terms of targeting effective application.

interaction_phrase: compared with other countries
occurrence_sentence: If we compare the Czech Republic with other countries in all contexts, such as the US, Australia, the UK, the Netherlands, Ireland, Denmark, or Spain, we see an opportunity to support the development of quantum technologies, especially in terms of targeting effective application.

interaction_phrase: the Czech Republic, its human and technological capacities
occurrence_sentence: The Czech quantum ecosystem must be effectively set up in relation to the size and capabilities of the Czech Republic, its human and technological capacities, and must credibly represent it as an important and reliable partner in the international arena.

interaction_phrase: within NATO
occurrence_sentence: Prepare call for support participation Czech experts and representatives in the decision-making structures of the quantum ecosystem within the EU, NATO (STO, DIANA, NIF), thereby ensuring greater influence for the Czech Republic and enabling more intensive involvement in the European and international quantum ecosystem.

interaction_phrase: cooperation with the Ministry of the Interior
occurrence_sentence: The development and integration of these technologies should be managed and coordinated at all levels of government, with the Ministry of Defense playing a key role in cooperation with the Ministry of the Interior, the National Cyber and Information Security Agency, and the intelligence services.

interaction_phrase: Office of the Government of the Czech Republic
occurrence_sentence: Office of the Government of the Czech Republic Pillar 6: Quantum technology infrastructure For Czechia, building a cutting-edge infrastructure for quantum technologies is essential not only in terms of scientific progress, but also for long-term competitiveness in the European and global context.

occurrence_sentence: The working group consists of a representative of the Minister for Science, Research and Innovation or a member of the government who acts as chair of the RVVI, or a representative appointed by them, a representative of the Ministry of Industry and Trade, a representative of the Ministry of Education, Youth and Sports, a representative of the Ministry of Finance, a representative of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs, a representative of the Ministry of Defense, a representative of the Ministry of the Interior, a representative of the National Cyber and Information Security Agency, a representative of the Technology Agency of the Czech Republic, a representative of the Grant Agency of the Czech Republic, a representative of the Czech Rectors' Conference, a representative of the Czech Academy of Sciences, a representative of the Confederation of Industry and Transport, a representative of the Chamber of Commerce, and a representative of the trade unions.
